title_en: The Meccan Period: Trials and Perseverance
content_en:
The thirteen years of the Makkan period — from the beginning of revelation until the migration to Madinah — were a time of extraordinary trial for the Prophet and the early Muslim community. Mubarakpuri's account of this period does justice to the severity of the persecution the Muslims endured while conveying the spiritual and moral qualities that sustained them through it, providing one of the most inspiring narratives in Islamic history.

The early Makkan mission was conducted quietly, with the Prophet inviting those closest to him first: Khadijah his wife, Ali ibn Abi Talib his young cousin, Zayd ibn Harithah his freed slave, and Abu Bakr his close friend. These early believers formed the nucleus of the community, and their conversions were followed by others in the first three years. The public proclamation of the message, which the Prophet began at the command of the verse 'So proclaim what you are commanded' (15:94), triggered the Quraysh opposition that would define the remainder of the Makkan period.

The persecution of the early Muslims varied in intensity depending on their social status. The Prophet himself, protected by his uncle Abu Talib's tribal standing, was subjected to mockery, social ostracism, and harassment but was initially physically protected. Those without tribal protection — Bilal ibn Rabah, Ammar ibn Yasir and his parents, Khabbab ibn al-Aratt, and many others — were subjected to physical torture of extreme cruelty. Bilal was dragged across the burning sands of Makkah with a stone on his chest; Sumayyah, Ammar's mother, was martyred by Abu Jahl — the first martyr in Islamic history.

Mubarakpuri details the two migrations to Abyssinia, where the Negus (Najashi) King provided refuge to the persecuted Muslims. The episode in which Ja'far ibn Abi Talib recited the opening of Surah Maryam to the Negus, who wept and declared that the description of Jesus in the Quran and in the Gospels differed by no more than a stick's distance, is one of the most moving encounters of the seerah. The Negus's protection of the Muslim refugees against the Quraysh delegation that came to reclaim them demonstrated that the message of Islam could speak across religious boundaries.

The years of boycott — when the Quraysh imposed a social and economic siege on the Prophet's clan and their allies in the Shi'b (valley) of Abu Talib — represent the nadir of the Makkan hardship. For two to three years, the community endured near-starvation while their kinsmen outside heard the cries of hungry children without being permitted to trade or interact. When the boycott was eventually broken, both Khadijah and Abu Talib died within months — the 'Year of Sorrow' that left the Prophet in his most vulnerable condition.

Yet through all these trials, the Prophet continued to preach, to care for his community, and to receive revelation that sustained and directed him. The Night Journey (Isra wal-Mi'raj), occurring in this period, represents the ultimate divine consolation — a direct experience of the divine presence and a confirmation of the prophetic mission at its most difficult hour.
content_id:
Tiga belas tahun periode Makkah — dari permulaan wahyu hingga hijrah ke Madinah — adalah masa cobaan yang luar biasa bagi Nabi ﷺ dan komunitas Muslim awal. Kisah Mubarakpuri tentang periode ini memberikan gambaran yang adil tentang keparahan penganiayaan yang ditanggung oleh kaum Muslim sambil menyampaikan kualitas-kualitas spiritual dan moral yang menopang mereka melaluinya, menghadirkan salah satu narasi paling menginspirasi dalam sejarah Islam.

Misi Makkah awal dilakukan secara diam-diam, dengan Nabi ﷺ mengundang mereka yang paling dekat dengannya terlebih dahulu: Khadijah istrinya, Ali bin Abi Thalib sepupunya yang masih muda, Zaid bin Haritsah mantan budaknya yang dibebaskan, dan Abu Bakr sahabat dekatnya. Para mukmin awal ini membentuk inti komunitas, dan perpindahan iman mereka diikuti oleh yang lain dalam tiga tahun pertama. Proklamasi publik tentang risalah, yang dimulai Nabi ﷺ atas perintah ayat 'Maka sampaikanlah secara terang-terangan apa yang diperintahkan kepadamu' (15:94), memicu perlawanan Quraisy yang akan mendefinisikan sisa periode Makkah.

Penganiayaan terhadap kaum Muslim awal bervariasi intensitasnya tergantung pada status sosial mereka. Nabi ﷺ sendiri, yang dilindungi oleh kedudukan suku pamannya Abu Thalib, dihadapkan pada ejekan, pengucilan sosial, dan gangguan tetapi awalnya dilindungi secara fisik. Mereka yang tidak memiliki perlindungan suku — Bilal bin Rabah, Ammar bin Yasir dan orang tuanya, Khabbab bin al-Aratt, dan banyak lainnya — dikenakan penyiksaan fisik yang sangat kejam. Bilal diseret melintasi pasir Makkah yang membara dengan batu di dadanya; Sumayyah, ibu Ammar, dimartirkan oleh Abu Jahal — syahid pertama dalam sejarah Islam.

Mubarakpuri menguraikan dua hijrah ke Abissinia, di mana Raja Najasyi memberikan perlindungan kepada kaum Muslim yang dianiaya. Episode di mana Ja'far bin Abi Thalib membacakan pembukaan Surah Maryam kepada Najasyi, yang menangis dan menyatakan bahwa deskripsi tentang Isa dalam Al-Quran dan Injil berbeda tidak lebih dari jarak sebuah tongkat, adalah salah satu perjumpaan paling mengharukan dalam sirah. Perlindungan Najasyi terhadap para pengungsi Muslim dari delegasi Quraisy yang datang untuk mengklaim kembali mereka membuktikan bahwa pesan Islam dapat berbicara melintasi batas-batas agama.

Tahun-tahun boikot — ketika Quraisy memberlakukan pengepungan sosial dan ekonomi terhadap klan Nabi dan sekutu-sekutu mereka di Syi'b (lembah) Abu Thalib — mewakili titik paling gelap dari kesulitan Makkah. Selama dua hingga tiga tahun, komunitas menanggung hampir kelaparan sementara sanak keluarga mereka di luar mendengar tangisan anak-anak yang lapar tanpa diizinkan untuk berdagang atau berinteraksi. Ketika boikot akhirnya dipatahkan, baik Khadijah maupun Abu Thalib wafat dalam beberapa bulan — 'Tahun Kesedihan' yang meninggalkan Nabi ﷺ dalam kondisi paling rentan.

Namun melalui semua cobaan ini, Nabi ﷺ terus berdakwah, merawat komunitasnya, dan menerima wahyu yang menopang dan mengarahkannya. Isra dan Mi'raj, yang terjadi pada periode ini, mewakili penghiburan ilahi tertinggi — pengalaman langsung kehadiran ilahi dan konfirmasi misi kenabian pada jam paling sulitnya.
content_ar: The thirteen years of the Makkan period — from the beginning of الوحي حتى the migration to المدينة المنورة — were a time of extraordinary الابتلاء for النبي ﷺ and the early مسلم الجماعة. Mubarakpuri's account of this period does العدل to the severity of the persecution the المسلمون endured بينما conveying the spiritual and moral qualities that sustained them through it, providing one of the most inspiring narratives in Islamic history. The early Makkan mission was conducted quietly, with النبي ﷺ inviting those closest to him أولاً: Khadijah his الزوجة, علي ibn Abi Talib his young cousin, Zayd ibn Harithah his freed slave, and أبو بكر his close friend. These early المؤمنون formed the nucleus of the الجماعة, and their conversions were followed by others in the أولاً three years. The public proclamation of the message, which النبي ﷺ began at the command of the الآية 'لذا proclaim what you are commanded' (15:94), triggered the Quraysh opposition that would define the remainder of the Makkan period. The persecution of the early المسلمون varied in intensity depending on their social status. النبي ﷺ himself, protected by his uncle Abu Talib's tribal standing, was subjected to mockery, social ostracism, and harassment but was initially physically protected. Those without tribal protection — Bilal ibn Rabah, Ammar ibn Yasir and his الوالدان, Khabbab ibn al-Aratt, and many others — were subjected to physical torture of extreme cruelty. Bilal was dragged across the burning sands of مكة المكرمة with a stone on his chest; Sumayyah, Ammar's الأم, was martyred by Abu Jahl — the أولاً martyr in Islamic history. Mubarakpuri details the two migrations to Abyssinia, where the Negus (Najashi) King provided refuge to the persecuted المسلمون. The episode in which Ja'far ibn Abi Talib recited the opening of السورة Maryam to the Negus, who wept and declared that the description of عيسى ﷺ in the القرآن الكريم and in the Gospels differed by no more than a stick's distance, is one of the most moving encounters of the seerah. The Negus's protection of the مسلم refugees against the Quraysh delegation that came to reclaim them demonstrated that the message of الإسلام could speak across religious boundaries. The years of boycott — عندما the Quraysh imposed a social and economic siege on النبي ﷺ's clan and their allies in the Shi'b (valley) of Abu Talib — represent the nadir of the Makkan الشدة. For two to three years, the الجماعة endured near-starvation بينما their kinsmen outside heard the cries of hungry الأولاد without being permitted to trade or interact. عندما the boycott was eventually broken, both Khadijah and Abu Talib died within months — the 'Year of Sorrow' that left النبي ﷺ in his most vulnerable condition. Yet through all these الابتلاءات, النبي ﷺ continued to preach, to care for his الجماعة, and to receive الوحي that sustained and directed him.
